personality_one_liner: A calming, reflective, and transparent space that feels human-made, helping users understand their stress without clinical anxiety.

spacing.rule: Generous vertical breathing room. Use soft asymmetry and intentional whitespace rather than rigid, dense grids.

do's: Use solid surfaceSecondary with slight opacity variations if depth is needed.
Use gradient scrims over background photos.
dont's: No frosted glass or heavy blur - clashes with the matte, natural paper aesthetic.

screens.name: Dashboard
screens.purpose: Overview of today's stress level (fused AI result) and quick actions to log a voice/text entry.
screens.layout: Soft blurred gradient background in the top 30% of the screen. A central hero card showing the latest prediction with a Level 1 Explainability badge (e.g., 'Predicted via Voice & Text'). Below, a 2-column layout for 'New Voice Entry' and 'New Text Entry' using rounded cards. Bottom section contains a simplified line chart of the week's stress levels.

states.empty: Empty state illustration with a warm prompt: 'Log your first voice note to see your stress trends.'
states.error: Gentle error message with a 'Retry' text button.

screens.name: Voice Recording
screens.purpose: A distraction-free, calming space to record voice thoughts for AI analysis.
screens.layout: Minimalist layout. A large circular record button at the bottom center with subtle, soft reanimated pulse rings while recording. The top displays a calm prompt ('How are you feeling right now?'). A sticky 'Analyze' button appears only after recording is stopped.

states.error: Microphone permission denied error with action to open settings.

screens.name: Analysis Results (Multimodal Fusion & XAI)
screens.purpose: Show the AI prediction (Low/Medium/High) and transparently explain why it made that decision.
screens.layout: Uses a 3-tier XAI pattern. The hero section shows the stress level. Below it, an expandable popover/card (Level 2) showing 3-5 key parameters in plain language (e.g., 'Elevated pitch detected in voice', 'Use of anxious words: "overwhelmed"'). An interactive heatmap highlights specific words from the text transcript. Includes a 'Download PDF Report' secondary button.

states.error: Failed to analyze data, please try again.

screens.name: History & Reports
screens.purpose: Chronological timeline of past entries with accessible PDF reports.
screens.layout: Vertical timeline layout using a left border and dots for each entry. Each item shows the time, stress level color-coded (success/warning/error colors but muted), and entry type (Voice/Text/Both). Sticky 'Export Monthly Report' at the bottom.

states.empty: Line art abstract plant illustration with 'Your journal is clear. Start by making an entry.'
states.error: Could not load history. Tap to retry.

instructions_to_main_agent: Use Personality 8 (Hand-Drawn / Journal LIGHT) as the core aesthetic. This app is for mental health and needs to feel extremely safe, tactile, and calming, avoiding any 'clinical' or 'tech-heavy' look.
Implement the UI with a strict ban on indigo, blue, purple, or pure black. Use the provided Sage/Dusty Olive brand colors and warm off-white/charcoal bases.
Use 'Fraunces' for headings (adds editorial warmth) and 'Nunito' for body text (soft and readable). Ensure maximum font weight is 500 across the app.

For the Explainable AI (XAI) UI, DO NOT use complex probability graphs. Implement a 3-tier structure: Level 1 indicator (badge), Level 2 plain language summary ('Why this prediction?'), and highlighted text for text analysis.
Ensure the voice recording screen has a prominent, easy-to-hit target and uses `react-native-reanimated` to create a soft, breathing pulse effect while recording.

For charts in the Dashboard, use the muted semantic colors (`success` for Low stress, `warning` for Medium, `error` for High). Ensure they blend with the soft journal aesthetic rather than looking like a corporate stock ticker.
Include gradient scrims (using expo-linear-gradient) over the dashboard_hero_bg image if text is overlaid on it to guarantee WCAG contrast.
Wire haptics for primary interactions as defined in the haptics block.
